description:
Advances: specled.branch_guard

Deliverable:
Scope-expansion ticket, carved by the orchestrator after specled_-s65.4's implementer diagnosed a genuine specled_ex bug blocking its own merge gate.

Root cause (independently confirmed by the orchestrator, file:line refs verified):
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex:121 computes the branch report's status as
  `"status" => if(findings == [], do: "pass", else: "fail")` — it ignores
  finding severity entirely. ANY finding, including a lone `:info`-severity
  finding, marks the branch report "fail".
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex:107 does `if branch_report["status"] == "fail" do`
  to decide whether `mix spec.check` exits non-zero. So an info-only finding
  makes the whole command exit 1.
- This directly contradicts .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md's own scenarios,
  e.g. `specled.branch_guard.scenario.file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades`
  (~line 220-233): "a branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding fires at
  severity `info`" + "mix spec.check exits zero" — the spec already promises
  this behavior; it is unimplemented.
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex:93-95 already implements the correct pattern for
  the OTHER report (SpecLedEx.verify/3's status): `errors = count(severity ==
  "error")`, `warnings = count(severity == "warning")`, `fail? = errors > 0 or
  (strict? and warnings > 0)`. branch_check.ex should mirror this severity-aware
  approach instead of its current finding-count check.
- Confirmed empirically: specled_-s65.4's implementer applied the blessed
  `Spec-Drift: test_only` trailer (downgrades branch_guard_missing_subject_update
  to `:info` per lib/specled_ex/branch_check/trailer.ex), and `mix spec.check`
  still exited 1 with only the info-severity finding present.
- The existing unit test test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs (~line 449,
  `file_touch_yields_to_attested_file` test) only asserts the finding's
  `severity` field equals `"info"` — it never asserts the report's `status`
  field or an end-to-end exit code, which is how this shipped unnoticed.

Fix:
- In lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, replace the finding-count status check
  with a severity-aware one, following the verifier.ex convention: count
  `severity == "error"` findings; the branch report fails only when that count
  is nonzero (branch_check has no `strict?` flag today — before deciding
  whether `:warning`-severity findings should also fail, read every scenario
  in .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md and .spec/specs/spec_drift_trailer.spec.md
  that names a `:warning`-severity finding and check whether any asserts
  "mix spec.check exits non-zero" for a warning-only case, e.g. via
  `.spec/config.yml`'s `branch_guard_untested_realization: warning` default —
  if such a scenario exists, warnings must also fail (do not silently change
  established behavior for that code); if no such scenario exists, only
  `:error`-severity findings should fail.
- Update or add a test that asserts the end-to-end `status`/exit behavior for
  an info-only finding (mirroring the exact case in
  `file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades`) — not just the finding's severity
  field. This is the assertion gap that let the original bug ship.
- If a spec-verification stub needs flipping from `execute: false` to
  `execute: true` to cover this end-to-end behavior, do so in
  .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md; do not otherwise edit spec prose beyond
  what's needed to keep the existing scenarios accurate.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: the updated/added test(s) in test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
  asserting severity-aware status computation (info-only -> status "pass",
  error-present -> status "fail", and the warning case per whatever the spec
  scenarios require).
- Behaviors to demonstrate: a branch with only an info-severity
  branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding (e.g. via a `Spec-Drift: test_only`
  trailer) makes `mix spec.check` exit 0, matching
  `specled.branch_guard.scenario.file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades`.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check/trailer.ex
- test/test_helper.exs
- .spec/config.yml
- .spec/specs/spec_drift_trailer.spec.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not change how findings are generated (severity resolution, trailer
  parsing) — only how the aggregate report status/exit code is derived from
  already-computed severities.
- Do not silently change exit behavior for any severity that an existing
  scenario says must fail (read every relevant scenario before deciding
  warning-severity behavior; do not guess).
- Do not touch test/test_helper.exs — that belongs to the sibling ticket
  specled_-s65.4, which is blocked on this fix.
- Conventions: escape interpolation in doc examples; @impl with explicit
  behaviour module; to_timeout/1 not :timer helpers.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: Fix branch_check status/exit code to be severity-aware (info/warning findings incorrectly fail mix spec.check)
